frozen_components: Basis vectors and level hypervectors (VSA primitives)
architecture.summary: A hybrid system combining transformer-based LLM embeddings with Vector Symbolic Architectures (VSA). Text and metadata from tweets are first encoded into real-valued semantic vectors via SentenceTransformers (768-dim), then projected into high-dimensional binary VSA space (1k–10k bits) via random projection. Metadata attributes (location, sentiment, timestamp, language) are separately encoded lexically or via ML classifiers, then combined using VSA binding (XOR) and bundling (majority-sum) operations to form compound tweet vectors. Matching is performed in VSA space using Hamming distance, with FAISS indexing for efficiency. A minimal metadata model bridges semantic and structured representations.
trained_components: Named Entity Recognition model (for location extraction)

biblio.title: Vector Symbolic Open Source Information Discovery
biblio.venue: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning for Multi-Domain Operations Applications VI, vol. 13051, pp. 380-390. SPIE, 2024

claims.statement: The approach combines transformer models for semantic matching with the compactness and representational structure of VSA, enabling data discovery in DDIL (denied, degraded, intermittent, low bandwidth) settings.
VSA binary vectors are typically 1-10k bits, suitable in DDIL settings, compared to transformer embeddings which are significantly larger (e.g., 768 real numbers for BERT).
Projecting LLM-derived semantic vectors (768 real numbers) to 1k bit VSA vectors results in compression.

For semantic matching (Russia-Ukraine, Stocks, Warthunder topics), multiple-vector 1k-bit representation achieved accuracy of 1.0, 0.993, and 0.997 respectively; single-vector 10k-bit achieved 1.0, 0.973, and 0.983.
Location and language lexical matching achieved 100% recall for both 1k and 10k bit representations across all possible values.
Sentiment classification accuracy was 95% for multiple-vector 1k-bit but dropped to 80% for single-vector 1k-bit; 10k-bit achieved 97% for both representations.

Timestamp ordering (level hypervectors method) achieved 99% accuracy for multiple-vector but degraded to close to random for single-vector 1k-bit; using basis vectors method, single-vector achieved 90% accuracy.

A minimal metadata model supporting four dimensions (temporal, spatial, semantic, and sentiment) can be captured in 4k bits (small enough to fit in a minimum-size IPv4 datagram) with good matching accuracy across all attributes.
